name: Sambar
recipeInstructions:
1. Use one medium-sized onion peeled and chopped (if you prefer).
2. Soak toor dhal in 4 cups water for one hour in a heavy-based pot.
3. Chop onions, potato, and tomato (green beans and carrots may also be added).
4. Add turmeric powder to the soaking dalh and place on stove.
5. Bring to boil, then lower heat to keep dalh simmering. Close the pot and allow it to cook until tender (about 30-45 minutes). Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
6. While dalh is cooking, lightly fry onions/shallots in 2.5 tbsp vegetable oil. Do not let them brown.
7. When dalh has cooked, add more water to bring the water level up to 4-5 cups (use your judgement).
8. Add potatoes, tomato, sautéed onions/shallots, and any other vegetables you want to use.
9. Add tamarind paste or tamarind "juice" and sambar powder. Stir and bring to a simmer.
10. Cover and allow the cook until vegetables are tender (about 15 minutes), stirring occasionally.
11. Heat remaining 1/2 tbsp oil and add mustard, cumin, coriander seeds, and crushed red chili to hot oil. Stir once when mustard seeds begin to pop and add to cooking sambar.
12. Finally, add cilantro leaves and cook for another 5 minutes before serving.
Sambar can be eaten with plain cooked rice, idlis, or dhosas. It is an integral part of South Indian cooking, often made daily.
